title: Ellisia racemosa
intro: Ellisia racemosa, also known as "Ellisia," is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Ellisia racemosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ellisia racemosa is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, often in combination with other herbs to support digestive health and alleviate gastrointestinal issues.
